texto: here’s a toast to Alan Turingborn in harsher, darker timeswho thought outside the containerand loved outside the linesand so the code-breaker was brokenand we’re sorryyes now the s-word has been spokenthe official conscience woken– very carefully scripted but at least it’s not encrypted –and the story does suggesta part 2 to the Turing Test:1. can machines behave like humans?2. can we?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Matt Harvey: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “here’s a toast to Alan Turingborn in harsher, darker timeswho thought outside the containerand lo…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, cryptography, cs pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e cs.

texto: I think there's a lot of learning process in figuring out what things you want to do and shouldn't do. Maturing in that way is something that comes with experience and time.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Matt Harvey sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “I think there's a lot of learning process in figuring out what things you want to do and shouldn'…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, time, experience, útil em momentos de experience e want.
